game_name: Might and Magic III: Isles of Terra

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Turn-based tactical combat with ranged and melee options established early
Grid-based navigation with automapping reduces navigation friction
Menu-based spell selection (improved from numeric codes in predecessors)
Enemies visible at range allows tactical preparation vs. previous surprise encounters

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Mid-game progression across Isles of Terra
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Large maps no longer limited to 16x16 grid; travel between separate nacelle worlds
Plot layers reveal Elemental Lords and Forces of the Dome
High-stakes combat without save capability increases tension
Major dungeon requiring sustained commitment without checkpoint safety
Ultimate test granting 'Ultimate Adventurers' title; grants access to Pyramids
Underwater seedship discovery; main control center reveals game world origins
Matter transferral mechanics and escape pod scenario bridge to MM IV

typical_relief_triggers: Visiting town/inn (Fountain Head and other safe havens)
Saving game state (available almost anytime outside Arena/Castle Greywind)
Defeating enemy encounters at distance before engagement
Autocombat resolution without mandatory looting search
typical_tension_triggers: Castle Greywind Dungeon traversal (no save capability)
Encounters with Sheltem's forces and Guardians
Navigation of large, unmapped nacelle worlds
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation from safe town exploration to save-disabled high-stakes dungeons; narrative-driven tension increases as party uncovers Corak's journals and pursues Guardians; endgame focuses intensity through mechanical restrictions (no saves) and cosmic stakes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible for both new and experienced players; Fountain Head serves as gentle introduction; distance-based enemy visibility reduces early punishment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive expansion across multiple nacelle worlds; increasing dungeon complexity; Guardian conflict escalates through narrative revelation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Significant spike with Arena and Castle Greywind (save-disabled zones); Maze from Hell presents ultimate test; Pyramids of Ancients endgame content

notable_level_patterns.description: Fountain Head and other towns serve as relief valves with save points, merchant services, and NPC exposition via Corak's journals
First-person grid-based exploration with increasing complexity; automapping reduces navigation friction but not combat difficulty
High-stakes areas (Arena, Castle Greywind) restrict save functionality to amplify tension and commitment
Separate nacelle worlds connected by travel mechanics; large maps (>16x16) requiring sustained exploration across diverse environments
Maze from Hell leads to Pyramids of Ancients; underwater seedship revelation ties gameplay to metagame narrative and MM IV setup
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Maze from Hell, Pyramids of Ancients
